title: TSA Workers Receive Back Pay — Airport Wait Times Plummet From 4+ Hours to Under 30 Minutes
detail: On March 30, TSA workers began receiving back pay covering roughly four weeks of missed wages after Trump signed a presidential memo directing DHS and OMB to pay TSA officers from available departmental funds. The effect was near-immediate: wait times at Atlanta Hartsfield-Jackson fell from over 90 minutes to under 3 minutes; Houston Bush Intercontinental dropped from 90 minutes to 9 minutes. LaGuardia still reported 2+ hour waits that morning but normalized by afternoon. The DHS shutdown itself remained unresolved — now in its 45th day, officially the longest partial government shutdown in U.S. history — as the Senate briefly convened on March 30 but departed for a two-week recess through April 13 without considering the House-passed stopgap. Border Czar Tom Homan stated ICE agents deployed to airports would remain until TSA returned to full operational strength. TSA had lost over 500 officers to attrition since the shutdown began February 14.

media.caption: Airport wait times plummet as TSA workers receive back pay

title: DHS Announces ICE Agent Rammed by Vehicle During Arrest Attempt in Sacramento
detail: DHS published a formal press release on March 30 detailing an incident from March 25 in Sacramento, California, in which ICE agents attempted to arrest Xa Lee, a Laotian national with prior felony convictions including felony firearm possession and two DUIs. Lee rammed his vehicle into an ICE officer and fled the scene. He remained at-large as of the press release. DHS characterized the attack as 'part of a disturbing trend of vehicle attacks on ICE officers' and called on Congress to pass stiffer penalties for assaulting federal immigration agents. The announcement came amid congressional debate over DHS funding and ICE accountability, with Democrats pushing for mandatory body cameras and use-of-force reporting requirements as conditions for any shutdown resolution.

title: 14th ICE Custody Death of 2026 Publicly Confirmed: Jose Guadalupe Ramos-Solano Dies at Adelanto
detail: DHS publicly confirmed on approximately March 30 the death of Jose Guadalupe Ramos-Solano, a Mexican national who was found unresponsive in his bunk at the Adelanto ICE Processing Center in California on March 25. He had been booked at Adelanto on February 23 with documented pre-existing conditions including diabetes, hyperlipidemia, and hypertension. He was transported to Victor Valley Global Medical Center where he was pronounced dead. His death was the 14th death in ICE custody in 2026 and the 47th since the start of the Trump second term in January 2025. Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum responded publicly, promising stronger diplomatic protests over conditions for Mexican nationals in ICE detention. Advocates highlighted that ICE had stopped paying for third-party medical treatment as of October 3, 2025, contributing to detention health crises.
